segments.text: Now it happened, as He went into the house of one of the rulers of the Pharisees to eat bread on the Sabbath, that they watched Him closely.
And behold, there was a certain man before Him who had dropsy.
And Jesus, answering, spoke to the lawyers and Pharisees, saying,
Is it lawful to heal on the Sabbath?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But they kept silent. And He took him and healed him, and let him go.
Then He answered them, saying,
Which of you, having a donkey or an ox that has fallen into a pit, will not immediately pull him out on the Sabbath day?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: And they could not answer Him regarding these things.
So He told a parable to those who were invited, when He noted how they chose the best places, saying to them:
When you are invited by anyone to a wedding feast, do not sit down in the best place, lest one more honorable than you be invited by him;
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: and he who invited you and him come and say to you, 'Give place to this man,' and then you begin with shame to take the lowest place.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But when you are invited, go and sit down in the lowest place, so that when he who invited you comes he may say to you, 'Friend, go up higher.' Then you will have glory in the presence of those who sit at the table with you.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: For whoever exalts himself will be humbled, and he who humbles himself will be exalted.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Then He also said to him who invited Him,
When you give a dinner or a supper, do not ask your friends, your brothers, your relatives, nor rich neighbors, lest they also invite you back, and you be repaid.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But when you give a feast, invite the poor, the maimed, the lame, the blind.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: And you will be blessed, because they cannot repay you; for you shall be repaid at the resurrection of the just.
segments.speaker: Jesus

verses.title: The Parable of the Great Supper

segments.text: Now when one of those who sat at the table with Him heard these things,
Blessed is he who shall eat bread in the kingdom of God!
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Then He said to him,
A certain man gave a great supper and invited many,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: and sent his servant at supper time to say to those who were invited, 'Come, for all things are now ready.'
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But they all with one accord began to make excuses.
I have bought a piece of ground, and I must go and see it. I ask you to have me excused.
segments.speaker: First Invited Guest

segments.text: I have bought five yoke of oxen, and I am going to test them. I ask you to have me excused.
segments.speaker: Second Invited Guest

segments.text: I have married a wife, and therefore I cannot come.
segments.speaker: Third Invited Guest

segments.text: So that servant came and reported these things to his master.
Go out quickly into the streets and lanes of the city, and bring in here the poor and the maimed and the lame and the blind.
segments.speaker: Master of the House

segments.text: Master, it is done as you commanded, and still there is room.
segments.speaker: Servant

segments.text: Go out into the highways and hedges, and compel them to come in, that my house may be filled.
segments.speaker: Master of the House

segments.text: For I say to you that none of those men who were invited shall taste my supper.
segments.speaker: Master of the House

segments.text: Now great multitudes went with Him. And He turned and said to them,
If anyone comes to Me and does not hate his father and mother, wife and children, brothers and sisters, yes, and his own life also, he cannot be My disciple.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: And whoever does not bear his cross and come after Me cannot be My disciple.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: For which of you, intending to build a tower, does not sit down first and count the cost, whether he has enough to finish it—
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: lest, after he has laid the foundation, and is not able to finish, all who see it begin to mock him,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: saying, 'This man began to build and was not able to finish'?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Or what king, going to make war against another king, does not sit down first and consider whether he is able with ten thousand to meet him who comes against him with twenty thousand?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Or else, while the other is still a great way off, he sends a delegation and asks conditions of peace.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: So likewise, whoever of you does not forsake all that he has cannot be My disciple.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Salt is good; but if the salt has lost its flavor, how shall it be seasoned?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: It is neither fit for the land nor for the dunghill, but men throw it out. He who has ears to hear, let him hear!
segments.speaker: Jesus
